_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Acute Kidney Injury and one or more of the indications for initiating RRT: hyperkalemia (> 6 mEq/L)
Acute Kidney Injury and one or more of the indications for initiating RRT: fluid overload (pulmonary edema, severe hypertension)
Acute Kidney Injury and one or more of the indications for initiating RRT: uremic encephalopathy
Acute Kidney Injury and one or more of the indications for initiating RRT: severe metabolic acidosis (TCO2 < 10–12 mEq/L)
Acute Kidney Injury and one or more of the indications for initiating RRT: hyponatremia (120 mEq/L or symptomatic) or hypernatremia
Patients with stage 5 CKD may have life-threatening biochemical abnormalities, requiring renal replacement therapies (RRT), [Kidney failure, end stage renal disease < GFR 15 (mL/min/1.73 m²)]

preauth.label: Notes showing evidence of unstable hemodynamic status

ppd_preauth.q: Is there a history of Acute kidney injury or chronic kidney disease with cardiovascular instability?
Is there an indication for CRRT?
Are other types of dialysis contraindicated?
Are admission notes showing vitals and examination findings present?
Are investigations for high creatinine present?
Are other necessary investigations (urinalysis, CBC, Sr. Electrolytes, LFT, ABG, Chest X-ray, Renal ultrasound, sepsis screen) present as per medical condition and availability?
Is the planned line of treatment documented?
Is documentation of consent from the guardian present?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) detailing history, clinical course and deteriorating condition present?
Are frequent monitoring charts (vital signs, labs, hemodynamics during treatment, procedure performance) present?
Is a detailed Discharge summary with follow-up advice at discharge present?

common_queries: History of acute or chronic kidney disease with cardiovascular instability not documented or unclear
Indication for CRRT not documented or not meeting clinical criteria
Other types of dialysis not documented as contraindicated or justification for CRRT not provided
Clinical notes not sufficiently detailing history, clinical course, deteriorating condition, or indication for procedure
High serum creatinine investigation not present or not adequately high to justify CRRT
Consent from guardian not documented or incomplete
Anticoagulation status not documented or not justified if contraindicated
Frequent monitoring charts (vitals, labs, hemodynamics) or procedure performance details missing/incomplete

additional_information.special_empanelment: Special expertise, equipment and trained staff in critical care dialysis (CRRT) and pediatric patient management. Care at Tertiary care hospital
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with CRRT only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation
Continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) is useful in patients with unstable hemodynamic status, concomitant sepsis, or multiorgan failure in the intensive care setting.
